name: HYPERDENSE INTRACRANIAL LESIONS
dominant_finding: ~ 30 mm above highest posterior elevation of pyramids
distribution: ~ 30 mm above highest posterior elevation of pyramids
discriminating_features: amorphous / ringlike calcification < 3 mm from midline usually < 10 mm in diameter
~ 30 mm above highest posterior elevation of pyramids
posteriorly open C-shaped calcification 4-6 mm anterior to pineal gland
20-30 mm behind + slightly below pineal on lateral projection, symmetrical on AP

general: amorphous / ringlike calcification < 3 mm from midline usually < 10 mm in diameter
~ 30 mm above highest posterior elevation of pyramids
posteriorly open C-shaped calcification 4-6 mm anterior to pineal gland
20-30 mm behind + slightly below pineal on lateral projection, symmetrical on AP

Astrocytomas calcify less frequently but are the most common tumor!
